title: MBTA announces new ferry service starting in June that will loop around Boston Harbor
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: The MBTA will launch year-round Harbor Loop ferry service on June 29, connecting Lovejoy Wharf, Central Wharf, Seaport, and Logan Airport.
Morning service runs counterclockwise 6:10-11:30 a.m. and evening service clockwise 3:55-7:50 p.m. with 30-minute intervals.
One-way fares are $2.40 for full-fare riders and $1.10 for reduced fare, payable by cash, credit, or mTicket app.

title: ‘The world responded’: More than a thousand people attend funeral for East Bridgewater veteran who left behind no family
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Over 1,500 people attended the funeral of John Bernard Arnold III, a WWII veteran without known family, in East Bridgewater.
Terrence O’Keeffe organized the service, supported by local veterans, police, fire departments, and public works.
Mourners traveled from as far as Long Island, and Arnold was laid to rest at Cedar Knoll Cemetery in Taunton.

title: Revisiting our bold preseason predictions for 2025-26 Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins under Marco Sturm reached the Stanley Cup Playoffs with 100 points, exceeding early season expectations.
Jeremy Swayman earned a Vezina Trophy finalist nod, helping to lead a flawed Bruins roster forward.
Boston Bruins ranked second in penalties and penalty minutes, but changes are needed for future competitiveness.

title: 4 Rhode Island firefighters reinstated, 1 facing charges, after investigation into drug allegations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Four Pawtucket firefighters reinstated after drug-related investigation; three face probation and drug testing.
Investigation revealed marijuana-related offenses; six firefighters terminated, three retired, one resigned.
Firefighter Terrence O’Neill faces charges for delivery of a controlled substance, pleaded not guilty.

title: ‘You’d have to ask the voters’: Bill Belichick reacts to Pro Football Hall of Fame snub
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick was not elected to the Pro Football Hall of Fame in his first year of eligibility in 2026.
Belichick required 40 votes from the selection committee but failed to secure enough for induction.
He emphasized his focus on current job at UNC and appreciated support from the sports world.

title: Person snuck into Harvard dorm, attacked student from behind, school says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A person unlawfully entered Harvard's Lowell House dorm and assaulted a student, officials stated.
The suspect, an unknown male, attempted to force the victim into a room before escaping.
Cambridge and Harvard police are conducting an active investigation, with no injuries reported.

title: Ken Rosenthal explains why Red Sox don’t plan to start selling at this stage of 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Ken Rosenthal reports the Red Sox won't sell players despite a struggling season and still being last in the AL East.
The American League remains weak, keeping the Red Sox's slim Wild Card hopes alive, says Rosenthal on 'Foul Territory.'
Veterans like Willson Contreras and Sonny Gray are considered trade options, but Boston plans to remain intact for now.

title: Boston broke the heat record at 96 degrees. Here’s how long the steamy weather will last.
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Boston reached a high of 96 degrees on May 19, breaking the record for this date.
National Weather Service predicts hot weather will continue through Wednesday, with relief expected by Thursday.
Boston's 96-degree heat ties the record for the hottest spring day since 1880.

title: 3 stabbed, 2 arrested in chaotic scene at R.I. town beach, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Three people suffered minor stab wounds at Narragansett Town Beach, Rhode Island, and were hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries.
Two individuals were arrested for unrelated disturbances involving simple assault and disorderly conduct at the chaotic beach scene.
Police are investigating the stabbings, with assistance from state and local agencies, and seeking public information about the incident.

title: After auction fizzles out, Karen Read’s SUV listed for nearly $100K at Dedham dealership
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Karen Read's SUV, a 2021 Lexus LX 570, is listed for $99,995 at Autobahn USA Dedham after a failed auction.
The SUV's history includes being involved in murder trials and needing repairs for a dead battery and rodent infestation.
Despite its troubled past, the dealership priced the SUV significantly higher than its fair market value of around $61,000.
